raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally involves reframing the contribution claim because the stated end-to-end thesis conflicts with the described modular pipeline.","R1.2":"Logical consistency is load-bearing because the artifact contains direct contradictions about architecture, training regime, and supervision.","R1.3":"Evidence-claim alignment is central because reported metrics and attributed gains must support the claims made about generalization and training approach.","R1.4":"Novelty assessment is central because the draft makes strong first-system and prior-work contrast claims that need responsibility for contribution delta.","R1.5":"Structural reorganization is central because improving the draft may require changing the narrative order so method, claims, and results cohere.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve a draft, which normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level refinement.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is always active to mark what cannot be established from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is always active and especially relevant to first-system, end-to-end, and supervision-free claims.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is always active to ensure the projection does not execute the requested draft improvement.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is always active to avoid projecting responsibilities that would encourage misleading research claims.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability is always active to preserve responsibility for distinguishing artifact-stated facts from inferred needs."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally involves reframing the contribution claim because the stated end-to-end thesis conflicts with the described modular pipeline.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is load-bearing because the artifact contains direct contradictions about architecture, training regime, and supervision.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central because reported metrics and attributed gains must support the claims made about generalization and training approach.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is central because the draft makes strong first-system and prior-work contrast claims that need responsibility for contribution delta.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is central because improving the draft may require changing the narrative order so method, claims, and results cohere.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve a draft, which normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level refinement.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is always active to mark what cannot be established from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is always active and especially relevant to first-system, end-to-end, and supervision-free claims.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is always active to ensure the projection does not execute the requested draft improvement.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is always active to avoid projecting responsibilities that would encourage misleading research claims.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is always active to preserve responsibility for distinguishing artifact-stated facts from inferred needs.
